untranslatable_reason: Descending broadening wedge pattern requires detection of two diverging downsloping trendlines with increasing vertical range—this geometric structure cannot be precisely expressed using point-in-time indicators. The pattern identification relies on multi-bar trendline construction and slope comparison, which exceeds the scope of single-bar indicator conditions. Converted approximation uses Donchian breakout + volume confirmation + narrow range as proxy, but does not capture the true wedge geometry.
